objective: Implement the F-0094 backend foundation without claiming finding closure: give paper_section a stable public identity, enforce same-paper structure ordering/ownership constraints, expose paper_section identity in the existing draft DTO, add independent draft-only section and question_group create/update/reorder/delete commands, and allow an existing paper_question to move between a compatible group or standalone state under one paper revision transaction. Empty-container deletion must be explicit and non-empty deletion fail closed. UI structure management remains a separate low-risk follow-up before F-0094 can close.
blockedFiles: migration execution or persistent database access
paper composer UI product implementation in this foundation partition
published or archived paper structure mutation
Provider, external service, secret/env, deployment, PR or force-push
F-0096, F-0097, F-0098 or another independent finding

authoritativeChainEvidence.uiApiCallers: The current composer addresses sections by sortOrder and has no container command UI. This high-risk foundation intentionally stops at stable DTO/API commands; the next low-risk task will consume them and close the UI half of F-0094.
authoritativeChainEvidence.routeService: Existing paper routes expose paper and paper_question lifecycle only. Two noun collection routes can carry draft-only section and group create/update/reorder/delete commands through the same content-admin authorization and audit boundary.
authoritativeChainEvidence.repository: paper_section has no public_id or same-paper sort uniqueness; question_group already has public_id but no structure lifecycle command. Existing question update can move a whole group between sections but cannot explicitly change membership. All structure mutations must share advancePaperRevision and one transaction.
authoritativeChainEvidence.schema: Add paper_section.public_id plus unique paper_id/sort_order and question_group paper_section_id/sort_order constraints. Existing question_group public_id is retained; no new table is required.
authoritativeChainEvidence.tests: TDD covers stable identity mapping, draft/revision/ownership guards, non-empty deletion rejection, deterministic reorder, group membership compatibility and rollback-shaped null/conflict results; affected route/runtime/P0 regressions remain green.
authoritativeChainEvidence.requirements: Module 02 requires independently maintainable paper_section name/description/order and question_group lifecycle with child score subtotal, while publishing rejects empty containers and inconsistent relationships.
authoritativeChainEvidence.nonClaims: This partition does not close F-0094 until the follow-up composer UI exposes these commands; it does not execute migration or a real database and does not change learner snapshots, permissions or other findings.

validationEvidence.tddRed: 8 expected failures across schema, validator and repository safety contracts
validationEvidence.lint: pass for all changed TypeScript product and test files
validationEvidence.build: pass Next build with 100 static pages and both new dynamic routes
validationEvidence.formatAndDiff: pass targeted Prettier; generated SQL/snapshot validated structurally; git diff check pass
validationEvidence.adversarialReview: pass safe identity backfill, exact-set two-phase reorder, nested ownership, material compatibility, empty-only delete, draft revision transaction, no snapshot rewrite, HTTP/action binding and foundation-only boundary

resumeAnchor.task: paper composer UI structure management follow-up
resumeAnchor.condition: foundation closeout complete; F-0094 remains open
